titleStmt.title: Letter from William J. Thompson to Sir Matthew Nathan, 28 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William J. Thompson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Sir William John Thompson (1861-1929) the Registrar General, to Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising. Thompson is enquire into the stage of a open trial of one Mr John Graham in connection with Birth Certificate forgery.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3908

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
General Register Office, Charlemont House, Dublin , 28th February, 1916. Sir, With reference to the letter of the 2nd instant from your
Department (No. 1706) with regard to the prosecution of Mr. John
Graham, Superintendent Registrar of Births, &c., Cootehill, on a
charge of forgery in connection with the issue of a false Birth
Certificate, I beg to say that, having regard to the near approach
of the Assizes, I would wish to be informed how the matter stands. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, William J. Thompson
Registrar General. The Under Secretary, &c., &c., Dublin Castle. Ask mr Smith
For report
EOF
29/2/16
